content.problemStatement:
Chefland is holding a virtual marathon for the categories 101010 km, 212121 km and 424242 km having prizes A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C (A<B<CA \lt B \lt CA<B<C) respectively to promote physical fitness. In order to claim the prize in a particular category the person must cover the total distance for that category within DDD days. Also a single person **cannot** apply in multiple categories.

Given the maximum distance ddd km that Chef can cover in a single day, find the maximum prize that Chef can win at the end of DDD days. If Chef can't win any prize, print 000.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, five integers D,d,A,B,CD, d, A, B, CD,d,A,B,C.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the answer to the problem.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: The maximum distance covered by Chef is 1⋅1=11 \cdot 1 = 11⋅1=1 km which is less than any of the available distance categories. Hence Chef won't be able to claim a prize in any of the categories.
Test Case 222: The maximum distance covered by Chef is 10⋅1=1010 \cdot 1 = 1010⋅1=10 km which is equal to distance of the first category. Hence Chef can claim the maximum prize of 111 units.
Test Case 333: The maximum distance covered by Chef is 10⋅3=3010 \cdot 3 = 3010⋅3=30 km which is more than the distance of the second category but less than that of the third category. Hence Chef can claim the maximum prize of 222 units.
